input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install .NET SDK

- Download from [dot.net](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest).
- Verify: `dotnet --version`
- Install the MAUI workload:
  ```bash
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` (should show `maui`)

### 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- Install from the **Mac App Store** (ensures code-signing & licensing).
- After install, run:
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

### 3. Install Android SDK (for Android)

The easiest path — install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio). Then via its **SDK Manager**, install:

- **Android SDK Platform** for your target API level (e.g., API 34/35)
- **Android SDK Build-Tools** (latest)
- **Android SDK Command-line Tools**
- **Android Emulator** + a system image for testing

Set environment variables (add to `~/.zshrc`):
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
```

Verify: `adb --version` and `emulator -list-avds`

### 4. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac was retired in Aug 2024. VS Code with the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions is now the official free path on macOS.

### 5. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See available SDK versions
dotnet sdk check

# See which workload versions are available
dotnet workload search maui

# See installed Android SDK components
sdkmanager --list   # inside Android SDK cmdline-tools/bin
```

Check the [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) and [support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/platform/support/policy/maui) for the compatibility matrix between .NET SDK, MAUI, Xcode, and Android API levels.

### 6. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<simulator-udid>
```

If all four build/run commands succeed, your environment is fully operational.

### Quick Checklist

1. ✅ .NET SDK installed → `dotnet --version`
2. ✅ MAUI workload installed → `dotnet workload list`
3. ✅ Xcode + CLI tools → `xcodebuild -version`
4. ✅ Android SDK + emulator → `adb --version`
5. ✅ `ANDROID_HOME` set → `echo $ANDROID_HOME`
6. ✅ Test project builds for both platforms
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They don't need me to run commands or explore a codebase — this is a knowledge/guidance question. I can answer this directly from my knowledge.
